title: Fix build error with GCC by forcing Clang in CMake on android/aarch64
body:
GCC does not recognize Clang-specific warning flags like -Wunreachable-code-break and -Wunreachable-code-return, which are passed by upstream submodules (e.g., ggml). This patch forces CMake to use Clang via command-line arguments, avoiding the need to patch nested submodules.

This resolves compiler errors without modifying submodule source code.

step_plan.summary: Fixes Android/aarch64 build failures caused by GCC rejecting Clang-only warning flags by updating the Python build driver to configure CMake with the Android NDK Clang toolchain (via command-line arguments) instead of patching upstream submodules.

steps.operation: Extend CLI to accept Android/NDK build parameters (target enablement + NDK path + ABI/API) that can be forwarded into the CMake configure invocation
steps.reason: Forcing Clang “via command-line arguments” requires a user-facing way to declare an Android/aarch64 build and provide the minimum Android toolchain inputs (NDK, ABI, platform/API) so `compile()` can construct the correct `cmake -B ...` arguments.
steps.side_effects: Adds/changes CLI surface area; callers/CI scripts may need updates. Must maintain backward compatibility for existing non-Android invocations and validate inputs (e.g., missing NDK path).

steps.operation: Adjust system/arch detection logic to support Android cross-build mode (avoid treating the Android target as host arch, map aarch64 to Android ABI arm64-v8a when requested)
steps.reason: Current host-centric arch detection (e.g., arm64 vs x86_64) can mis-drive feature toggles and build flags when cross-compiling. Android/aarch64 should be handled as a target configuration (ABI) rather than relying on host architecture, and it must line up with the toolchain inputs used by CMake.
steps.side_effects: May affect any downstream logic that uses `system_info()` for selecting options (e.g., BitNet-related toggles). Ensure non-Android behavior remains unchanged; add conservative defaults so existing host builds do not regress.

steps.operation: Inject Android toolchain + Clang-forcing CMake configure arguments when targeting Android/aarch64 (e.g., android.toolchain.cmake + ANDROID_ABI/ANDROID_PLATFORM + explicit C/CXX compiler overrides if needed)
steps.reason: The build failure occurs at CMake configure/build time when GCC is selected and upstream submodules add Clang-only warning flags. The most targeted fix is to ensure the Android NDK Clang toolchain is used by passing the Android toolchain file and (defensively) `-DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=clang` / `-DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=clang++` (or NDK-prefixed clang binaries) as command-line `-D` arguments for Android arm64 builds.
steps.side_effects: Changes the generated CMake cache for Android builds; may require clearing the build directory when switching between toolchains. Could impact users who intentionally used non-NDK toolchains; document override behavior and ensure the new flags are only applied for Android/aarch64 mode. May require CI to install/point to Android NDK and set API level/ABI.
